(I) (2) These rules may be called the Export of Milk Products (Quality Control, Inspection and Monitoring) Amendment Rules, 2004, They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Ofiicial Gazette. rule 2 of the Export of Milk Products (Quality Control, Inspection and Monitoring ) Rules, 2000, for clause x), the following clause shall be substituted, namel :-~ “(x) “Ultla High Temperature (UHT) Treatment” is treatment of milk or cream in a high temperature or short time heat treatment aimej at producing a commercially sterile product, which can be stored at room temperature. Note—— The UI- under normal 5 sealed contains rninimnm F0 V combination w Note: The prin and ame. IT process aims to destroy all micro organisms. Any residual micro organism is unlikely to cause spoilage orage conditions The UHT-treated milk and cream are packaged aseptically into sterilized, hermetically rs. The total heat treatment is equivalent, in terms of its effectiveness against heat resistant spores, to a lue of three minutes, The temperature for UI-IT treatment is in the range of I35 degree—150 degree C in th appropriate holding times such as 140 V for 2—3 seconds.” ' {E No. 6/ l/2000-EI&EP] M. V. P. C. SASTRY, Jt, Secy. :ipal rules were published in the Gazette Of India, vide number SO. 2720 dated 28-1 1-2000 and subsequently .ded vide SO. 3719 dated 12-11'—2002.
